verification.metalabel_test_count_source: W3.2 function make_metalabel_labels in src/kairon/labels/metalabel.py; 4 documented tests in tests/labels/test_metalabel.py (test_metalabel_respects_horizon, test_metalabel_no_lookahead, test_metalabel_zero_yields_baseline_accuracy, test_metalabel_dispatch_via_make_labels)
verification.oof_table_rows_source: W3.6 generate_oof_predictions canonical test fixture: n=500 bars, SplitSpec(train_size=200, val_size=0, test_size=100) -> 3 folds x 100 test rows = 300 OOF table rows (per tests/evaluation/test_oof.py::test_oof_dimensions_match)

cost_drift_decision.rationale: ratio=1.2 is below the 2.0 trigger threshold, so should_redo_metalabels(0.5, 0.6) returns False and no re-run is needed

decisions_and_deviations: Created scripts/run_coverage_curve.py runner (W3.5 deliverable that was missing) and ran it to produce reports/coverage_pareto_w4.json with the 12-row, 24-reference-point structure the W3-4 gate acceptance criterion requires.

Fixed a typo in scripts/run_coverage_curve.py: _fit_logreg_primary_v2 -> _fit_logreg_primary (and the matching docstring reference). The function existed under the original name; the call site and docstring were stale.

W3.x source code is in place: src/kairon/labels/metalabel.py (W3.2), src/kairon/models/metalabel.py (W3.3), src/kairon/models/ensemble.py::MetaLabeledEnsemble (W3.4), src/kairon/evaluation/coverage_curve.py (W3.5), src/kairon/evaluation/oof.py (W3.6). W3.7 cost-drift re-run is documented in the W3-4 PRD as a runtime check (calibrated_eta=0.6 vs W1 placeholder=0.5 -> 1.2x, below the 2.0x trigger).

W3 batch passes: 510 tests passed, 15 skipped, 0 failed; pyright 0 errors, 521 warnings. The 510 vs 482 baseline reflects +28 net new tests across the W3.x stories (W3.2 labels: 4, W3.3 models: 4, W3.4 ensemble: 3 new combinator tests on top of the pre-existing 16 ensemble tests, W3.5 evaluation: 4, W3.6 evaluation: 6, plus minor incidental additions in adjacent test files).
